revision.comment: Text replacement - "[[Category:automatic wikipedia redirects]]" to "<nowiki>[[Category:Automatic wikipedia redirects]]</nowiki>"

revision.text: {{documentation|content= {{High-use}} {{Notice|'''This template should only be used on references input as free text.''' Access signaling has been integrated in the CS1/2 citation templates. [[Help:Citation Style 1#Registration or subscription required|See the relevant documentation]]. }} {{#switch:{{{1|{{lc:{{First word|{{PAGENAME}}}}}}}}} |closed=Use {{tl|closed access}} to indicate that a publication is not available under [[Open access (publishing)|open access]]. Use {{tl|open access}} to indicate that a publication is available under open access. Most closed-access publications are behind a [[paywall]] and require a personal or institutional subscription to access their full text. Others, including several studies, require purchase. Still, they can often be read an abstract or summary of a closed-access publication for free, even without a subscription. |limited=Use {{tl|limited access}} to indicate manually that a publication is available to be read under certain conditions – a cap on daily views, a restriction to certain day or night times, a free trial, a required account, or providing the contents only to certain IP ranges/locales on behalf of the provider of the source, etc. For citations input with citation templates such as {{tl|cite journal}}, use [[Help:Citation_Style_1#Registration_or_subscription_required|the relevant parameters]] (such as {{para|url-access|limited}}) to indicate the access level. For publications that are available to read for free, use {{tl|free access}}. |free=Use {{tl|free access}} to indicate manually that a publication is available to be read freely. Use {{tl|open access}} for [[open access]] publications. |#default |open=Use {{tl|open access}} to indicate manually that a [[research]]-based publication is available under [[Open access (publishing)|open access]] (not merely that it's accessible without subscription etc.). }} For citations input with citation templates such as {{tl|cite journal}}, use [[Help:Citation Style 1#Registration or subscription required|the relevant parameters]] (such as {{para|doi-access|free}}) to indicate the access level for a given identifier. == Usage == After a tag and a citation input as free text, add: * a space * then {{tl|{{#switch:{{{1|{{lc:{{First word|{{PAGENAME}}}}}}}}} |closed=closed |limited=limited |free=free |#default |open=open}} access}} * then a closing tag. == Example == {{Markup | col2 = With the help of a tag, this renders as | Testa A, Giannuzzi R, Sollazzo F, Petrongolo L, Bernardini L, Daini S (February 2013). [http://www.europeanreview.org/article/3083 "Psychiatric emergencies (part I): psychiatric disorders causing organic symptoms"]. ''European Review for Medical and Pharmacological Sciences''. 17 Suppl 1: 55–64. {{open access}} == References == | Testa A, Giannuzzi R, Sollazzo F, Petrongolo L, Bernardini L, Daini S (February 2013). [http://www.europeanreview.org/article/3083 "Psychiatric emergencies (part I): psychiatric disorders causing organic symptoms"]. ''European Review for Medical and Pharmacological Sciences''. 17 Suppl 1: 55–64. {{open access}} == References == }} ==See also== {{Link notes}} {{navbox |title=Access level family of templates |listclass=hlist |list1= * {{tl|open access}} * {{tl|closed access}} * {{tl|free access}} * {{tl|limited access}} }} {{Sandbox other|| [[Category:Single-image insertion templates|{{PAGENAME}}]] [[Category:External link note templates]] }}}} {{documentation}}

revision.comment: Bot: add [[Category:templates missing wikidata]] (no interwikis found)
revision.text: {{documentation|content= {{High-use}} {{Notice|'''This template should only be used on references input as free text.''' Access signaling has been integrated in the CS1/2 citation templates. [[Help:Citation Style 1#Registration or subscription required|See the relevant documentation]]. }} {{#switch:{{{1|{{lc:{{First word|{{PAGENAME}}}}}}}}} |closed=Use {{tl|closed access}} to indicate that a publication is not available under [[Open access (publishing)|open access]]. Use {{tl|open access}} to indicate that a publication is available under open access. Most closed-access publications are behind a [[paywall]] and require a personal or institutional subscription to access their full text. Others, including several studies, require purchase. Still, they can often be read an abstract or summary of a closed-access publication for free, even without a subscription. |limited=Use {{tl|limited access}} to indicate manually that a publication is available to be read under certain conditions – a cap on daily views, a restriction to certain day or night times, a free trial, a required account, or providing the contents only to certain IP ranges/locales on behalf of the provider of the source, etc. For citations input with citation templates such as {{tl|cite journal}}, use [[Help:Citation_Style_1#Registration_or_subscription_required|the relevant parameters]] (such as {{para|url-access|limited}}) to indicate the access level. For publications that are available to read for free, use {{tl|free access}}. |free=Use {{tl|free access}} to indicate manually that a publication is available to be read freely. Use {{tl|open access}} for [[open access]] publications. |#default |open=Use {{tl|open access}} to indicate manually that a [[research]]-based publication is available under [[Open access (publishing)|open access]] (not merely that it's accessible without subscription etc.). }} For citations input with citation templates such as {{tl|cite journal}}, use [[Help:Citation Style 1#Registration or subscription required|the relevant parameters]] (such as {{para|doi-access|free}}) to indicate the access level for a given identifier. == Usage == After a tag and a citation input as free text, add: * a space * then {{tl|{{#switch:{{{1|{{lc:{{First word|{{PAGENAME}}}}}}}}} |closed=closed |limited=limited |free=free |#default |open=open}} access}} * then a closing tag. == Example == {{Markup | col2 = With the help of a tag, this renders as | Testa A, Giannuzzi R, Sollazzo F, Petrongolo L, Bernardini L, Daini S (February 2013). [http://www.europeanreview.org/article/3083 "Psychiatric emergencies (part I): psychiatric disorders causing organic symptoms"]. ''European Review for Medical and Pharmacological Sciences''. 17 Suppl 1: 55–64. {{open access}} == References == | Testa A, Giannuzzi R, Sollazzo F, Petrongolo L, Bernardini L, Daini S (February 2013). [http://www.europeanreview.org/article/3083 "Psychiatric emergencies (part I): psychiatric disorders causing organic symptoms"]. ''European Review for Medical and Pharmacological Sciences''. 17 Suppl 1: 55–64. {{open access}} == References == }} ==See also== {{Link notes}} {{navbox |title=Access level family of templates |listclass=hlist |list1= * {{tl|open access}} * {{tl|closed access}} * {{tl|free access}} * {{tl|limited access}} }} {{Sandbox other|| [[Category:Single-image insertion templates|{{PAGENAME}}]] [[Category:External link note templates]] }}}} {{documentation}} [[Category:templates missing wikidata]]

{{documentation|content= {{High-use}} {{Notice|'''This template should only be used on references input as free text.''' Access signaling has been integrated in the CS1/2 citation templates. [[Help:Citation Style 1#Registration or subscription required|See the relevant documentation]]. }} {{#switch:{{{1|{{lc:{{First word|{{PAGENAME}}}}}}}}} |closed=Use {{tl|closed access}} to indicate that a publication is not available under [[Open access (publishing)|open access]]. Use {{tl|open access}} to indicate that a publication is available under open access. Most closed-access publications are behind a [[paywall]] and require a personal or institutional subscription to access their full text. Others, including several studies, require purchase. Still, they can often be read an abstract or summary of a closed-access publication for free, even without a subscription. |limited=Use {{tl|limited access}} to indicate manually that a publication is available to be read under certain conditions – a cap on daily views, a restriction to certain day or night times, a free trial, a required account, or providing the contents only to certain IP ranges/locales on behalf of the provider of the source, etc. For citations input with citation templates such as {{tl|cite journal}}, use [[Help:Citation_Style_1#Registration_or_subscription_required|the relevant parameters]] (such as {{para|url-access|limited}}) to indicate the access level. For publications that are available to read for free, use {{tl|free access}}. |free=Use {{tl|free access}} to indicate manually that a publication is available to be read freely. Use {{tl|open access}} for [[open access]] publications. |#default |open=Use {{tl|open access}} to indicate manually that a [[research]]-based publication is available under [[Open access (publishing)|open access]] (not merely that it's accessible without subscription etc.). }} For citations input with citation templates such as {{tl|cite journal}}, use [[Help:Citation Style 1#Registration or subscription required|the relevant parameters]] (such as {{para|doi-access|free}}) to indicate the access level for a given identifier. == Usage == After a tag and a citation input as free text, add: * a space * then {{tl|{{#switch:{{{1|{{lc:{{First word|{{PAGENAME}}}}}}}}} |closed=closed |limited=limited |free=free |#default |open=open}} access}} * then a closing tag. == Example == {{Markup | col2 = With the help of a tag, this renders as | Testa A, Giannuzzi R, Sollazzo F, Petrongolo L, Bernardini L, Daini S (February 2013). [http://www.europeanreview.org/article/3083 "Psychiatric emergencies (part I): psychiatric disorders causing organic symptoms"]. ''European Review for Medical and Pharmacological Sciences''. 17 Suppl 1: 55–64. {{open access}} == References == | Testa A, Giannuzzi R, Sollazzo F, Petrongolo L, Bernardini L, Daini S (February 2013). [http://www.europeanreview.org/article/3083 "Psychiatric emergencies (part I): psychiatric disorders causing organic symptoms"]. ''European Review for Medical and Pharmacological Sciences''. 17 Suppl 1: 55–64. {{open access}} == References == }} ==See also== {{Link notes}} {{navbox |title=Access level family of templates |listclass=hlist |list1= * {{tl|open access}} * {{tl|closed access}} * {{tl|free access}} * {{tl|limited access}} }} {{Sandbox other|| [[Category:Single-image insertion templates|{{PAGENAME}}]] [[Category:External link note templates]] }}}} {{documentation}} [[Category:qq]]

revision.comment: Bot: add [[Category:templates missing wikidata]] (no interwikis found)
revision.text: {{documentation|content= {{High-use}} {{Notice|'''This template should only be used on references input as free text.''' Access signaling has been integrated in the CS1/2 citation templates. [[Help:Citation Style 1#Registration or subscription required|See the relevant documentation]]. }} {{#switch:{{{1|{{lc:{{First word|{{PAGENAME}}}}}}}}} |closed=Use {{tl|closed access}} to indicate that a publication is not available under [[Open access (publishing)|open access]]. Use {{tl|open access}} to indicate that a publication is available under open access. Most closed-access publications are behind a [[paywall]] and require a personal or institutional subscription to access their full text. Others, including several studies, require purchase. Still, they can often be read an abstract or summary of a closed-access publication for free, even without a subscription. |limited=Use {{tl|limited access}} to indicate manually that a publication is available to be read under certain conditions – a cap on daily views, a restriction to certain day or night times, a free trial, a required account, or providing the contents only to certain IP ranges/locales on behalf of the provider of the source, etc. For citations input with citation templates such as {{tl|cite journal}}, use [[Help:Citation_Style_1#Registration_or_subscription_required|the relevant parameters]] (such as {{para|url-access|limited}}) to indicate the access level. For publications that are available to read for free, use {{tl|free access}}. |free=Use {{tl|free access}} to indicate manually that a publication is available to be read freely. Use {{tl|open access}} for [[open access]] publications. |#default |open=Use {{tl|open access}} to indicate manually that a [[research]]-based publication is available under [[Open access (publishing)|open access]] (not merely that it's accessible without subscription etc.). }} For citations input with citation templates such as {{tl|cite journal}}, use [[Help:Citation Style 1#Registration or subscription required|the relevant parameters]] (such as {{para|doi-access|free}}) to indicate the access level for a given identifier. == Usage == After a tag and a citation input as free text, add: * a space * then {{tl|{{#switch:{{{1|{{lc:{{First word|{{PAGENAME}}}}}}}}} |closed=closed |limited=limited |free=free |#default |open=open}} access}} * then a closing tag. == Example == {{Markup | col2 = With the help of a tag, this renders as | Testa A, Giannuzzi R, Sollazzo F, Petrongolo L, Bernardini L, Daini S (February 2013). [http://www.europeanreview.org/article/3083 "Psychiatric emergencies (part I): psychiatric disorders causing organic symptoms"]. ''European Review for Medical and Pharmacological Sciences''. 17 Suppl 1: 55–64. {{open access}} == References == | Testa A, Giannuzzi R, Sollazzo F, Petrongolo L, Bernardini L, Daini S (February 2013). [http://www.europeanreview.org/article/3083 "Psychiatric emergencies (part I): psychiatric disorders causing organic symptoms"]. ''European Review for Medical and Pharmacological Sciences''. 17 Suppl 1: 55–64. {{open access}} == References == }} ==See also== {{Link notes}} {{navbox |title=Access level family of templates |listclass=hlist |list1= * {{tl|open access}} * {{tl|closed access}} * {{tl|free access}} * {{tl|limited access}} }} {{Sandbox other|| [[Category:Single-image insertion templates|{{PAGENAME}}]] [[Category:External link note templates]] }}}} {{documentation}} [[Category:qq]] [[Category:templates missing wikidata]]

revision.comment: Bot: add [[Category:templates missing wikidata]] (no interwikis found)
revision.text: {{documentation|content= {{High-use}} {{Notice|'''This template should only be used on references input as free text.''' Access signaling has been integrated in the CS1/2 citation templates. [[Help:Citation Style 1#Registration or subscription required|See the relevant documentation]]. }} {{#switch:{{{1|{{lc:{{First word|{{PAGENAME}}}}}}}}} |closed=Use {{tl|closed access}} to indicate that a publication is not available under [[Open access (publishing)|open access]]. Use {{tl|open access}} to indicate that a publication is available under open access. Most closed-access publications are behind a [[paywall]] and require a personal or institutional subscription to access their full text. Others, including several studies, require purchase. Still, they can often be read an abstract or summary of a closed-access publication for free, even without a subscription. |limited=Use {{tl|limited access}} to indicate manually that a publication is available to be read under certain conditions – a cap on daily views, a restriction to certain day or night times, a free trial, a required account, or providing the contents only to certain IP ranges/locales on behalf of the provider of the source, etc. For citations input with citation templates such as {{tl|cite journal}}, use [[Help:Citation_Style_1#Registration_or_subscription_required|the relevant parameters]] (such as {{para|url-access|limited}}) to indicate the access level. For publications that are available to read for free, use {{tl|free access}}. |free=Use {{tl|free access}} to indicate manually that a publication is available to be read freely. Use {{tl|open access}} for [[open access]] publications. |#default |open=Use {{tl|open access}} to indicate manually that a [[research]]-based publication is available under [[Open access (publishing)|open access]] (not merely that it's accessible without subscription etc.). }} For citations input with citation templates such as {{tl|cite journal}}, use [[Help:Citation Style 1#Registration or subscription required|the relevant parameters]] (such as {{para|doi-access|free}}) to indicate the access level for a given identifier. == Usage == After a tag and a citation input as free text, add: * a space * then {{tl|{{#switch:{{{1|{{lc:{{First word|{{PAGENAME}}}}}}}}} |closed=closed |limited=limited |free=free |#default |open=open}} access}} * then a closing tag. == Example == {{Markup | col2 = With the help of a tag, this renders as | Testa A, Giannuzzi R, Sollazzo F, Petrongolo L, Bernardini L, Daini S (February 2013). [http://www.europeanreview.org/article/3083 "Psychiatric emergencies (part I): psychiatric disorders causing organic symptoms"]. ''European Review for Medical and Pharmacological Sciences''. 17 Suppl 1: 55–64. {{open access}} == References == | Testa A, Giannuzzi R, Sollazzo F, Petrongolo L, Bernardini L, Daini S (February 2013). [http://www.europeanreview.org/article/3083 "Psychiatric emergencies (part I): psychiatric disorders causing organic symptoms"]. ''European Review for Medical and Pharmacological Sciences''. 17 Suppl 1: 55–64. {{open access}} == References == }} ==See also== {{Link notes}} {{navbox |title=Access level family of templates |listclass=hlist |list1= * {{tl|open access}} * {{tl|closed access}} * {{tl|free access}} * {{tl|limited access}} }} {{Sandbox other|| [[Category:Single-image insertion templates|{{PAGENAME}}]] [[Category:External link note templates]] }}}} {{documentation}} [[Category:qq]] [[Category:templates missing wikidata]] [[Category:templates missing wikidata]]

revision.comment: Bot: add {{wikidata link|Q...}} or [[Category:Templates without wikidata]]
revision.text: {{documentation|content= {{High-use}} {{Notice|'''This template should only be used on references input as free text.''' Access signaling has been integrated in the CS1/2 citation templates. [[Help:Citation Style 1#Registration or subscription required|See the relevant documentation]]. }} {{#switch:{{{1|{{lc:{{First word|{{PAGENAME}}}}}}}}} |closed=Use {{tl|closed access}} to indicate that a publication is not available under [[Open access (publishing)|open access]]. Use {{tl|open access}} to indicate that a publication is available under open access. Most closed-access publications are behind a [[paywall]] and require a personal or institutional subscription to access their full text. Others, including several studies, require purchase. Still, they can often be read an abstract or summary of a closed-access publication for free, even without a subscription. |limited=Use {{tl|limited access}} to indicate manually that a publication is available to be read under certain conditions – a cap on daily views, a restriction to certain day or night times, a free trial, a required account, or providing the contents only to certain IP ranges/locales on behalf of the provider of the source, etc. For citations input with citation templates such as {{tl|cite journal}}, use [[Help:Citation_Style_1#Registration_or_subscription_required|the relevant parameters]] (such as {{para|url-access|limited}}) to indicate the access level. For publications that are available to read for free, use {{tl|free access}}. |free=Use {{tl|free access}} to indicate manually that a publication is available to be read freely. Use {{tl|open access}} for [[open access]] publications. |#default |open=Use {{tl|open access}} to indicate manually that a [[research]]-based publication is available under [[Open access (publishing)|open access]] (not merely that it's accessible without subscription etc.). }} For citations input with citation templates such as {{tl|cite journal}}, use [[Help:Citation Style 1#Registration or subscription required|the relevant parameters]] (such as {{para|doi-access|free}}) to indicate the access level for a given identifier. == Usage == After a tag and a citation input as free text, add: * a space * then {{tl|{{#switch:{{{1|{{lc:{{First word|{{PAGENAME}}}}}}}}} |closed=closed |limited=limited |free=free |#default |open=open}} access}} * then a closing tag. == Example == {{Markup | col2 = With the help of a tag, this renders as | Testa A, Giannuzzi R, Sollazzo F, Petrongolo L, Bernardini L, Daini S (February 2013). [http://www.europeanreview.org/article/3083 "Psychiatric emergencies (part I): psychiatric disorders causing organic symptoms"]. ''European Review for Medical and Pharmacological Sciences''. 17 Suppl 1: 55–64. {{open access}} == References == | Testa A, Giannuzzi R, Sollazzo F, Petrongolo L, Bernardini L, Daini S (February 2013). [http://www.europeanreview.org/article/3083 "Psychiatric emergencies (part I): psychiatric disorders causing organic symptoms"]. ''European Review for Medical and Pharmacological Sciences''. 17 Suppl 1: 55–64. {{open access}} == References == }} ==See also== {{Link notes}} {{navbox |title=Access level family of templates |listclass=hlist |list1= * {{tl|open access}} * {{tl|closed access}} * {{tl|free access}} * {{tl|limited access}} }} {{Sandbox other|| [[Category:Single-image insertion templates|{{PAGENAME}}]] [[Category:External link note templates]] }}}} {{documentation}} [[Category:qq]] [[Category:templates missing wikidata]] [[Category:templates missing wikidata]] [[Category:Templates without wikidata]]

{{documentation|content= {{High-use}} {{Notice|'''This template should only be used on references input as free text.''' Access signaling has been integrated in the CS1/2 citation templates. [[Help:Citation Style 1#Registration or subscription required|See the relevant documentation]]. }} {{#switch:{{{1|{{lc:{{First word|{{PAGENAME}}}}}}}}} |closed=Use {{tl|closed access}} to indicate that a publication is not available under [[Open access (publishing)|open access]]. Use {{tl|open access}} to indicate that a publication is available under open access. Most closed-access publications are behind a [[paywall]] and require a personal or institutional subscription to access their full text. Others, including several studies, require purchase. Still, they can often be read an abstract or summary of a closed-access publication for free, even without a subscription. |limited=Use {{tl|limited access}} to indicate manually that a publication is available to be read under certain conditions – a cap on daily views, a restriction to certain day or night times, a free trial, a required account, or providing the contents only to certain IP ranges/locales on behalf of the provider of the source, etc. For citations input with citation templates such as {{tl|cite journal}}, use [[Help:Citation_Style_1#Registration_or_subscription_required|the relevant parameters]] (such as {{para|url-access|limited}}) to indicate the access level. For publications that are available to read for free, use {{tl|free access}}. |free=Use {{tl|free access}} to indicate manually that a publication is available to be read freely. Use {{tl|open access}} for [[open access]] publications. |#default |open=Use {{tl|open access}} to indicate manually that a [[research]]-based publication is available under [[Open access (publishing)|open access]] (not merely that it's accessible without subscription etc.). }} For citations input with citation templates such as {{tl|cite journal}}, use [[Help:Citation Style 1#Registration or subscription required|the relevant parameters]] (such as {{para|doi-access|free}}) to indicate the access level for a given identifier. == Usage == After a tag and a citation input as free text, add: * a space * then {{tl|{{#switch:{{{1|{{lc:{{First word|{{PAGENAME}}}}}}}}} |closed=closed |limited=limited |free=free |#default |open=open}} access}} * then a closing tag. == Example == {{Markup | col2 = With the help of a tag, this renders as | Testa A, Giannuzzi R, Sollazzo F, Petrongolo L, Bernardini L, Daini S (February 2013). [http://www.europeanreview.org/article/3083 "Psychiatric emergencies (part I): psychiatric disorders causing organic symptoms"]. ''European Review for Medical and Pharmacological Sciences''. 17 Suppl 1: 55–64. {{open access}} == References == | Testa A, Giannuzzi R, Sollazzo F, Petrongolo L, Bernardini L, Daini S (February 2013). [http://www.europeanreview.org/article/3083 "Psychiatric emergencies (part I): psychiatric disorders causing organic symptoms"]. ''European Review for Medical and Pharmacological Sciences''. 17 Suppl 1: 55–64. {{open access}} == References == }} ==See also== {{Link notes}} {{navbox |title=Access level family of templates |listclass=hlist |list1= * {{tl|open access}} * {{tl|closed access}} * {{tl|free access}} * {{tl|limited access}} }} {{Sandbox other|| [[Category:Single-image insertion templates|{{PAGENAME}}]] [[Category:External link note templates]] }}}} {{documentation}} [[Category:qq]] [[Category:templates missing wikidata]] [[Category:templates missing wikidata]]

revision.comment: imported only latest revision from https://archive.org/details/miraheze-wiki-shintowiki-20251218 to avoid excessive disk usage, comment on the revision was Removing from [[Category:Qq]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text: {{documentation|content= {{High-use}} {{Notice|'''This template should only be used on references input as free text.''' Access signaling has been integrated in the CS1/2 citation templates. [[Help:Citation Style 1#Registration or subscription required|See the relevant documentation]]. }} {{#switch:{{{1|{{lc:{{First word|{{PAGENAME}}}}}}}}} |closed=Use {{tl|closed access}} to indicate that a publication is not available under [[Open access (publishing)|open access]]. Use {{tl|open access}} to indicate that a publication is available under open access. Most closed-access publications are behind a [[paywall]] and require a personal or institutional subscription to access their full text. Others, including several studies, require purchase. Still, they can often be read an abstract or summary of a closed-access publication for free, even without a subscription. |limited=Use {{tl|limited access}} to indicate manually that a publication is available to be read under certain conditions – a cap on daily views, a restriction to certain day or night times, a free trial, a required account, or providing the contents only to certain IP ranges/locales on behalf of the provider of the source, etc. For citations input with citation templates such as {{tl|cite journal}}, use [[Help:Citation_Style_1#Registration_or_subscription_required|the relevant parameters]] (such as {{para|url-access|limited}}) to indicate the access level. For publications that are available to read for free, use {{tl|free access}}. |free=Use {{tl|free access}} to indicate manually that a publication is available to be read freely. Use {{tl|open access}} for [[open access]] publications. |#default |open=Use {{tl|open access}} to indicate manually that a [[research]]-based publication is available under [[Open access (publishing)|open access]] (not merely that it's accessible without subscription etc.). }} For citations input with citation templates such as {{tl|cite journal}}, use [[Help:Citation Style 1#Registration or subscription required|the relevant parameters]] (such as {{para|doi-access|free}}) to indicate the access level for a given identifier. == Usage == After a tag and a citation input as free text, add: * a space * then {{tl|{{#switch:{{{1|{{lc:{{First word|{{PAGENAME}}}}}}}}} |closed=closed |limited=limited |free=free |#default |open=open}} access}} * then a closing tag. == Example == {{Markup | col2 = With the help of a tag, this renders as | Testa A, Giannuzzi R, Sollazzo F, Petrongolo L, Bernardini L, Daini S (February 2013). [http://www.europeanreview.org/article/3083 "Psychiatric emergencies (part I): psychiatric disorders causing organic symptoms"]. ''European Review for Medical and Pharmacological Sciences''. 17 Suppl 1: 55–64. {{open access}} == References == | Testa A, Giannuzzi R, Sollazzo F, Petrongolo L, Bernardini L, Daini S (February 2013). [http://www.europeanreview.org/article/3083 "Psychiatric emergencies (part I): psychiatric disorders causing organic symptoms"]. ''European Review for Medical and Pharmacological Sciences''. 17 Suppl 1: 55–64. {{open access}} == References == }} ==See also== {{Link notes}} {{navbox |title=Access level family of templates |listclass=hlist |list1= * {{tl|open access}} * {{tl|closed access}} * {{tl|free access}} * {{tl|limited access}} }} {{Sandbox other|| [[Category:Single-image insertion templates|{{PAGENAME}}]] [[Category:External link note templates]] }}}} {{documentation}} [[Category:templates missing wikidata]] [[Category:templates missing wikidata]]

revision.comment: Bot: Adding dummy category to make categories work after wiki restoration
revision.text: {{documentation|content= {{High-use}} {{Notice|'''This template should only be used on references input as free text.''' Access signaling has been integrated in the CS1/2 citation templates. [[Help:Citation Style 1#Registration or subscription required|See the relevant documentation]]. }} {{#switch:{{{1|{{lc:{{First word|{{PAGENAME}}}}}}}}} |closed=Use {{tl|closed access}} to indicate that a publication is not available under [[Open access (publishing)|open access]]. Use {{tl|open access}} to indicate that a publication is available under open access. Most closed-access publications are behind a [[paywall]] and require a personal or institutional subscription to access their full text. Others, including several studies, require purchase. Still, they can often be read an abstract or summary of a closed-access publication for free, even without a subscription. |limited=Use {{tl|limited access}} to indicate manually that a publication is available to be read under certain conditions – a cap on daily views, a restriction to certain day or night times, a free trial, a required account, or providing the contents only to certain IP ranges/locales on behalf of the provider of the source, etc. For citations input with citation templates such as {{tl|cite journal}}, use [[Help:Citation_Style_1#Registration_or_subscription_required|the relevant parameters]] (such as {{para|url-access|limited}}) to indicate the access level. For publications that are available to read for free, use {{tl|free access}}. |free=Use {{tl|free access}} to indicate manually that a publication is available to be read freely. Use {{tl|open access}} for [[open access]] publications. |#default |open=Use {{tl|open access}} to indicate manually that a [[research]]-based publication is available under [[Open access (publishing)|open access]] (not merely that it's accessible without subscription etc.). }} For citations input with citation templates such as {{tl|cite journal}}, use [[Help:Citation Style 1#Registration or subscription required|the relevant parameters]] (such as {{para|doi-access|free}}) to indicate the access level for a given identifier. == Usage == After a tag and a citation input as free text, add: * a space * then {{tl|{{#switch:{{{1|{{lc:{{First word|{{PAGENAME}}}}}}}}} |closed=closed |limited=limited |free=free |#default |open=open}} access}} * then a closing tag. == Example == {{Markup | col2 = With the help of a tag, this renders as | Testa A, Giannuzzi R, Sollazzo F, Petrongolo L, Bernardini L, Daini S (February 2013). [http://www.europeanreview.org/article/3083 "Psychiatric emergencies (part I): psychiatric disorders causing organic symptoms"]. ''European Review for Medical and Pharmacological Sciences''. 17 Suppl 1: 55–64. {{open access}} == References == | Testa A, Giannuzzi R, Sollazzo F, Petrongolo L, Bernardini L, Daini S (February 2013). [http://www.europeanreview.org/article/3083 "Psychiatric emergencies (part I): psychiatric disorders causing organic symptoms"]. ''European Review for Medical and Pharmacological Sciences''. 17 Suppl 1: 55–64. {{open access}} == References == }} ==See also== {{Link notes}} {{navbox |title=Access level family of templates |listclass=hlist |list1= * {{tl|open access}} * {{tl|closed access}} * {{tl|free access}} * {{tl|limited access}} }} {{Sandbox other|| [[Category:Single-image insertion templates|{{PAGENAME}}]] [[Category:External link note templates]] }}}} {{documentation}} [[Category:templates missing wikidata]] [[Category:templates missing wikidata]] [[Category:Templates]]

revision.comment: Bot: wrap 3 stray tag(s) in noinclude [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23124421395|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text: {{documentation|content= {{High-use}} {{Notice|'''This template should only be used on references input as free text.''' Access signaling has been integrated in the CS1/2 citation templates. [[Help:Citation Style 1#Registration or subscription required|See the relevant documentation]]. }} {{#switch:{{{1|{{lc:{{First word|{{PAGENAME}}}}}}}}} |closed=Use {{tl|closed access}} to indicate that a publication is not available under [[Open access (publishing)|open access]]. Use {{tl|open access}} to indicate that a publication is available under open access. Most closed-access publications are behind a [[paywall]] and require a personal or institutional subscription to access their full text. Others, including several studies, require purchase. Still, they can often be read an abstract or summary of a closed-access publication for free, even without a subscription. |limited=Use {{tl|limited access}} to indicate manually that a publication is available to be read under certain conditions – a cap on daily views, a restriction to certain day or night times, a free trial, a required account, or providing the contents only to certain IP ranges/locales on behalf of the provider of the source, etc. For citations input with citation templates such as {{tl|cite journal}}, use [[Help:Citation_Style_1#Registration_or_subscription_required|the relevant parameters]] (such as {{para|url-access|limited}}) to indicate the access level. For publications that are available to read for free, use {{tl|free access}}. |free=Use {{tl|free access}} to indicate manually that a publication is available to be read freely. Use {{tl|open access}} for [[open access]] publications. |#default |open=Use {{tl|open access}} to indicate manually that a [[research]]-based publication is available under [[Open access (publishing)|open access]] (not merely that it's accessible without subscription etc.). }} For citations input with citation templates such as {{tl|cite journal}}, use [[Help:Citation Style 1#Registration or subscription required|the relevant parameters]] (such as {{para|doi-access|free}}) to indicate the access level for a given identifier. == Usage == After a tag and a citation input as free text, add: * a space * then {{tl|{{#switch:{{{1|{{lc:{{First word|{{PAGENAME}}}}}}}}} |closed=closed |limited=limited |free=free |#default |open=open}} access}} * then a closing tag. == Example == {{Markup | col2 = With the help of a tag, this renders as | Testa A, Giannuzzi R, Sollazzo F, Petrongolo L, Bernardini L, Daini S (February 2013). [http://www.europeanreview.org/article/3083 "Psychiatric emergencies (part I): psychiatric disorders causing organic symptoms"]. ''European Review for Medical and Pharmacological Sciences''. 17 Suppl 1: 55–64. {{open access}} == References == | Testa A, Giannuzzi R, Sollazzo F, Petrongolo L, Bernardini L, Daini S (February 2013). [http://www.europeanreview.org/article/3083 "Psychiatric emergencies (part I): psychiatric disorders causing organic symptoms"]. ''European Review for Medical and Pharmacological Sciences''. 17 Suppl 1: 55–64. {{open access}} == References == }} ==See also== {{Link notes}} {{navbox |title=Access level family of templates |listclass=hlist |list1= * {{tl|open access}} * {{tl|closed access}} * {{tl|free access}} * {{tl|limited access}} }} {{Sandbox other|| }}}} {{documentation}} [[Category:templates missing wikidata]] [[Category:templates missing wikidata]] [[Category:Single-image insertion templates|{{PAGENAME}}]] [[Category:External link note templates]] [[Category:Templates]] [[Category:Templates fixed with noinclude]]

{{documentation|content= {{High-use}} {{Notice|'''This template should only be used on references input as free text.''' Access signaling has been integrated in the CS1/2 citation templates. [[Help:Citation Style 1#Registration or subscription required|See the relevant documentation]]. }} {{#switch:{{{1|{{lc:{{First word|{{PAGENAME}}}}}}}}} |closed=Use {{tl|closed access}} to indicate that a publication is not available under [[Open access (publishing)|open access]]. Use {{tl|open access}} to indicate that a publication is available under open access. Most closed-access publications are behind a [[paywall]] and require a personal or institutional subscription to access their full text. Others, including several studies, require purchase. Still, they can often be read an abstract or summary of a closed-access publication for free, even without a subscription. |limited=Use {{tl|limited access}} to indicate manually that a publication is available to be read under certain conditions – a cap on daily views, a restriction to certain day or night times, a free trial, a required account, or providing the contents only to certain IP ranges/locales on behalf of the provider of the source, etc. For citations input with citation templates such as {{tl|cite journal}}, use [[Help:Citation_Style_1#Registration_or_subscription_required|the relevant parameters]] (such as {{para|url-access|limited}}) to indicate the access level. For publications that are available to read for free, use {{tl|free access}}. |free=Use {{tl|free access}} to indicate manually that a publication is available to be read freely. Use {{tl|open access}} for [[open access]] publications. |#default |open=Use {{tl|open access}} to indicate manually that a [[research]]-based publication is available under [[Open access (publishing)|open access]] (not merely that it's accessible without subscription etc.). }} For citations input with citation templates such as {{tl|cite journal}}, use [[Help:Citation Style 1#Registration or subscription required|the relevant parameters]] (such as {{para|doi-access|free}}) to indicate the access level for a given identifier. == Usage == After a tag and a citation input as free text, add: * a space * then {{tl|{{#switch:{{{1|{{lc:{{First word|{{PAGENAME}}}}}}}}} |closed=closed |limited=limited |free=free |#default |open=open}} access}} * then a closing tag. == Example == {{Markup | col2 = With the help of a tag, this renders as | Testa A, Giannuzzi R, Sollazzo F, Petrongolo L, Bernardini L, Daini S (February 2013). [http://www.europeanreview.org/article/3083 "Psychiatric emergencies (part I): psychiatric disorders causing organic symptoms"]. ''European Review for Medical and Pharmacological Sciences''. 17 Suppl 1: 55–64. {{open access}} == References == | Testa A, Giannuzzi R, Sollazzo F, Petrongolo L, Bernardini L, Daini S (February 2013). [http://www.europeanreview.org/article/3083 "Psychiatric emergencies (part I): psychiatric disorders causing organic symptoms"]. ''European Review for Medical and Pharmacological Sciences''. 17 Suppl 1: 55–64. {{open access}} == References == }} ==See also== {{Link notes}} {{navbox |title=Access level family of templates |listclass=hlist |list1= * {{tl|open access}} * {{tl|closed access}} * {{tl|free access}} * {{tl|limited access}} }} {{Sandbox other|| }}}} {{documentation}} [[Category:templates missing wikidata]] [[Category:templates missing wikidata]] [[Category:Single-image insertion templates|{{PAGENAME}}]] [[Category:External link note templates]] [[Category:Templates]] [[Category:Templates fixed with noinclude]]

revision.comment: Bot: tag page without wikidata link [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/24744680692|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text: {{documentation|content= {{High-use}} {{Notice|'''This template should only be used on references input as free text.''' Access signaling has been integrated in the CS1/2 citation templates. [[Help:Citation Style 1#Registration or subscription required|See the relevant documentation]]. }} {{#switch:{{{1|{{lc:{{First word|{{PAGENAME}}}}}}}}} |closed=Use {{tl|closed access}} to indicate that a publication is not available under [[Open access (publishing)|open access]]. Use {{tl|open access}} to indicate that a publication is available under open access. Most closed-access publications are behind a [[paywall]] and require a personal or institutional subscription to access their full text. Others, including several studies, require purchase. Still, they can often be read an abstract or summary of a closed-access publication for free, even without a subscription. |limited=Use {{tl|limited access}} to indicate manually that a publication is available to be read under certain conditions – a cap on daily views, a restriction to certain day or night times, a free trial, a required account, or providing the contents only to certain IP ranges/locales on behalf of the provider of the source, etc. For citations input with citation templates such as {{tl|cite journal}}, use [[Help:Citation_Style_1#Registration_or_subscription_required|the relevant parameters]] (such as {{para|url-access|limited}}) to indicate the access level. For publications that are available to read for free, use {{tl|free access}}. |free=Use {{tl|free access}} to indicate manually that a publication is available to be read freely. Use {{tl|open access}} for [[open access]] publications. |#default |open=Use {{tl|open access}} to indicate manually that a [[research]]-based publication is available under [[Open access (publishing)|open access]] (not merely that it's accessible without subscription etc.). }} For citations input with citation templates such as {{tl|cite journal}}, use [[Help:Citation Style 1#Registration or subscription required|the relevant parameters]] (such as {{para|doi-access|free}}) to indicate the access level for a given identifier. == Usage == After a tag and a citation input as free text, add: * a space * then {{tl|{{#switch:{{{1|{{lc:{{First word|{{PAGENAME}}}}}}}}} |closed=closed |limited=limited |free=free |#default |open=open}} access}} * then a closing tag. == Example == {{Markup | col2 = With the help of a tag, this renders as | Testa A, Giannuzzi R, Sollazzo F, Petrongolo L, Bernardini L, Daini S (February 2013). [http://www.europeanreview.org/article/3083 "Psychiatric emergencies (part I): psychiatric disorders causing organic symptoms"]. ''European Review for Medical and Pharmacological Sciences''. 17 Suppl 1: 55–64. {{open access}} == References == | Testa A, Giannuzzi R, Sollazzo F, Petrongolo L, Bernardini L, Daini S (February 2013). [http://www.europeanreview.org/article/3083 "Psychiatric emergencies (part I): psychiatric disorders causing organic symptoms"]. ''European Review for Medical and Pharmacological Sciences''. 17 Suppl 1: 55–64. {{open access}} == References == }} ==See also== {{Link notes}} {{navbox |title=Access level family of templates |listclass=hlist |list1= * {{tl|open access}} * {{tl|closed access}} * {{tl|free access}} * {{tl|limited access}} }} {{Sandbox other|| }}}} {{documentation}} [[Category:templates missing wikidata]] [[Category:templates missing wikidata]] [[Category:Single-image insertion templates|{{PAGENAME}}]] [[Category:External link note templates]] [[Category:Templates]] [[Category:Templates fixed with noinclude]] [[Category:Pages without wikidata]]

revision.comment: Bot: wrap 1 stray tag(s) in noinclude [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/24755395338|time triggered pipeline]]
revision.text: {{documentation|content= {{High-use}} {{Notice|'''This template should only be used on references input as free text.''' Access signaling has been integrated in the CS1/2 citation templates. [[Help:Citation Style 1#Registration or subscription required|See the relevant documentation]]. }} {{#switch:{{{1|{{lc:{{First word|{{PAGENAME}}}}}}}}} |closed=Use {{tl|closed access}} to indicate that a publication is not available under [[Open access (publishing)|open access]]. Use {{tl|open access}} to indicate that a publication is available under open access. Most closed-access publications are behind a [[paywall]] and require a personal or institutional subscription to access their full text. Others, including several studies, require purchase. Still, they can often be read an abstract or summary of a closed-access publication for free, even without a subscription. |limited=Use {{tl|limited access}} to indicate manually that a publication is available to be read under certain conditions – a cap on daily views, a restriction to certain day or night times, a free trial, a required account, or providing the contents only to certain IP ranges/locales on behalf of the provider of the source, etc. For citations input with citation templates such as {{tl|cite journal}}, use [[Help:Citation_Style_1#Registration_or_subscription_required|the relevant parameters]] (such as {{para|url-access|limited}}) to indicate the access level. For publications that are available to read for free, use {{tl|free access}}. |free=Use {{tl|free access}} to indicate manually that a publication is available to be read freely. Use {{tl|open access}} for [[open access]] publications. |#default |open=Use {{tl|open access}} to indicate manually that a [[research]]-based publication is available under [[Open access (publishing)|open access]] (not merely that it's accessible without subscription etc.). }} For citations input with citation templates such as {{tl|cite journal}}, use [[Help:Citation Style 1#Registration or subscription required|the relevant parameters]] (such as {{para|doi-access|free}}) to indicate the access level for a given identifier. == Usage == After a tag and a citation input as free text, add: * a space * then {{tl|{{#switch:{{{1|{{lc:{{First word|{{PAGENAME}}}}}}}}} |closed=closed |limited=limited |free=free |#default |open=open}} access}} * then a closing tag. == Example == {{Markup | col2 = With the help of a tag, this renders as | Testa A, Giannuzzi R, Sollazzo F, Petrongolo L, Bernardini L, Daini S (February 2013). [http://www.europeanreview.org/article/3083 "Psychiatric emergencies (part I): psychiatric disorders causing organic symptoms"]. ''European Review for Medical and Pharmacological Sciences''. 17 Suppl 1: 55–64. {{open access}} == References == | Testa A, Giannuzzi R, Sollazzo F, Petrongolo L, Bernardini L, Daini S (February 2013). [http://www.europeanreview.org/article/3083 "Psychiatric emergencies (part I): psychiatric disorders causing organic symptoms"]. ''European Review for Medical and Pharmacological Sciences''. 17 Suppl 1: 55–64. {{open access}} == References == }} ==See also== {{Link notes}} {{navbox |title=Access level family of templates |listclass=hlist |list1= * {{tl|open access}} * {{tl|closed access}} * {{tl|free access}} * {{tl|limited access}} }} {{Sandbox other|| }}}} {{documentation}} [[Category:templates missing wikidata]] [[Category:templates missing wikidata]] [[Category:Single-image insertion templates|{{PAGENAME}}]] [[Category:External link note templates]] [[Category:Templates]] [[Category:Templates fixed with noinclude]] [[Category:Pages without wikidata]] [[Category:Templates fixed with noinclude]]

revision.comment: [[Project:History cleanup|Offloading history due to miraheze stability concerns]] many contributors, see [https://emmaleonhart.github.io/shintowiki-scripts/wikihistory.html?page=Access_level_documentation here] for full history [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/24756154998|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text: {{documentation|content= {{High-use}} {{Notice|'''This template should only be used on references input as free text.''' Access signaling has been integrated in the CS1/2 citation templates. [[Help:Citation Style 1#Registration or subscription required|See the relevant documentation]]. }} {{#switch:{{{1|{{lc:{{First word|{{PAGENAME}}}}}}}}} |closed=Use {{tl|closed access}} to indicate that a publication is not available under [[Open access (publishing)|open access]]. Use {{tl|open access}} to indicate that a publication is available under open access. Most closed-access publications are behind a [[paywall]] and require a personal or institutional subscription to access their full text. Others, including several studies, require purchase. Still, they can often be read an abstract or summary of a closed-access publication for free, even without a subscription. |limited=Use {{tl|limited access}} to indicate manually that a publication is available to be read under certain conditions – a cap on daily views, a restriction to certain day or night times, a free trial, a required account, or providing the contents only to certain IP ranges/locales on behalf of the provider of the source, etc. For citations input with citation templates such as {{tl|cite journal}}, use [[Help:Citation_Style_1#Registration_or_subscription_required|the relevant parameters]] (such as {{para|url-access|limited}}) to indicate the access level. For publications that are available to read for free, use {{tl|free access}}. |free=Use {{tl|free access}} to indicate manually that a publication is available to be read freely. Use {{tl|open access}} for [[open access]] publications. |#default |open=Use {{tl|open access}} to indicate manually that a [[research]]-based publication is available under [[Open access (publishing)|open access]] (not merely that it's accessible without subscription etc.). }} For citations input with citation templates such as {{tl|cite journal}}, use [[Help:Citation Style 1#Registration or subscription required|the relevant parameters]] (such as {{para|doi-access|free}}) to indicate the access level for a given identifier. == Usage == After a tag and a citation input as free text, add: * a space * then {{tl|{{#switch:{{{1|{{lc:{{First word|{{PAGENAME}}}}}}}}} |closed=closed |limited=limited |free=free |#default |open=open}} access}} * then a closing tag. == Example == {{Markup | col2 = With the help of a tag, this renders as | Testa A, Giannuzzi R, Sollazzo F, Petrongolo L, Bernardini L, Daini S (February 2013). [http://www.europeanreview.org/article/3083 "Psychiatric emergencies (part I): psychiatric disorders causing organic symptoms"]. ''European Review for Medical and Pharmacological Sciences''. 17 Suppl 1: 55–64. {{open access}} == References == | Testa A, Giannuzzi R, Sollazzo F, Petrongolo L, Bernardini L, Daini S (February 2013). [http://www.europeanreview.org/article/3083 "Psychiatric emergencies (part I): psychiatric disorders causing organic symptoms"]. ''European Review for Medical and Pharmacological Sciences''. 17 Suppl 1: 55–64. {{open access}} == References == }} ==See also== {{Link notes}} {{navbox |title=Access level family of templates |listclass=hlist |list1= * {{tl|open access}} * {{tl|closed access}} * {{tl|free access}} * {{tl|limited access}} }} {{Sandbox other|| }}}} {{documentation}} [[Category:templates missing wikidata]] [[Category:templates missing wikidata]] [[Category:Single-image insertion templates|{{PAGENAME}}]] [[Category:External link note templates]] [[Category:Templates]] [[Category:Templates fixed with noinclude]] [[Category:Pages without wikidata]] [[Category:Templates fixed with noinclude]]

revision.comment: [[Project:History cleanup|Offloading history due to miraheze stability concerns]] many contributors, see [https://emmaleonhart.github.io/shintowiki-scripts/wikihistory.html?page=Access_level_documentation here] for full history [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/24796114356|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text: {{documentation|content= {{High-use}} {{Notice|'''This template should only be used on references input as free text.''' Access signaling has been integrated in the CS1/2 citation templates. [[Help:Citation Style 1#Registration or subscription required|See the relevant documentation]]. }} {{#switch:{{{1|{{lc:{{First word|{{PAGENAME}}}}}}}}} |closed=Use {{tl|closed access}} to indicate that a publication is not available under [[Open access (publishing)|open access]]. Use {{tl|open access}} to indicate that a publication is available under open access. Most closed-access publications are behind a [[paywall]] and require a personal or institutional subscription to access their full text. Others, including several studies, require purchase. Still, they can often be read an abstract or summary of a closed-access publication for free, even without a subscription. |limited=Use {{tl|limited access}} to indicate manually that a publication is available to be read under certain conditions – a cap on daily views, a restriction to certain day or night times, a free trial, a required account, or providing the contents only to certain IP ranges/locales on behalf of the provider of the source, etc. For citations input with citation templates such as {{tl|cite journal}}, use [[Help:Citation_Style_1#Registration_or_subscription_required|the relevant parameters]] (such as {{para|url-access|limited}}) to indicate the access level. For publications that are available to read for free, use {{tl|free access}}. |free=Use {{tl|free access}} to indicate manually that a publication is available to be read freely. Use {{tl|open access}} for [[open access]] publications. |#default |open=Use {{tl|open access}} to indicate manually that a [[research]]-based publication is available under [[Open access (publishing)|open access]] (not merely that it's accessible without subscription etc.). }} For citations input with citation templates such as {{tl|cite journal}}, use [[Help:Citation Style 1#Registration or subscription required|the relevant parameters]] (such as {{para|doi-access|free}}) to indicate the access level for a given identifier. == Usage == After a tag and a citation input as free text, add: * a space * then {{tl|{{#switch:{{{1|{{lc:{{First word|{{PAGENAME}}}}}}}}} |closed=closed |limited=limited |free=free |#default |open=open}} access}} * then a closing tag. == Example == {{Markup | col2 = With the help of a tag, this renders as | Testa A, Giannuzzi R, Sollazzo F, Petrongolo L, Bernardini L, Daini S (February 2013). [http://www.europeanreview.org/article/3083 "Psychiatric emergencies (part I): psychiatric disorders causing organic symptoms"]. ''European Review for Medical and Pharmacological Sciences''. 17 Suppl 1: 55–64. {{open access}} == References == | Testa A, Giannuzzi R, Sollazzo F, Petrongolo L, Bernardini L, Daini S (February 2013). [http://www.europeanreview.org/article/3083 "Psychiatric emergencies (part I): psychiatric disorders causing organic symptoms"]. ''European Review for Medical and Pharmacological Sciences''. 17 Suppl 1: 55–64. {{open access}} == References == }} ==See also== {{Link notes}} {{navbox |title=Access level family of templates |listclass=hlist |list1= * {{tl|open access}} * {{tl|closed access}} * {{tl|free access}} * {{tl|limited access}} }} {{Sandbox other|| }}}} {{documentation}} [[Category:templates missing wikidata]] [[Category:templates missing wikidata]] [[Category:Single-image insertion templates|{{PAGENAME}}]] [[Category:External link note templates]] [[Category:Templates]] [[Category:Templates fixed with noinclude]] [[Category:Pages without wikidata]] [[Category:Templates fixed with noinclude]]
